evidence_quotes.quote: I also urge the inclusion of game preservation provisions.
Many games are killed when servers shut down, leaving paying customers with nothing.
If a game is discontinued, players should have the right to an offline version or other viable access.

paragraphs.text: The Digital Fairness Act has real potential to improve the online experience by tackling manipulative dark patterns, hidden costs, gambling-like lootboxes, and fake in-game currencies. Clear, EU-wide rules with strong enforcement can make digital services more transparent and fair for everyone. The wording must be precise to avoid unintended consequences, such as privacy-invasive age verification systems or overcomplicated compliance that makes everyday digital life worse. Rules also need to be designed to prevent loopholes that allow harmful practices to reappear under a different name or through technical workarounds. I also urge the inclusion of game preservation provisions. Many games are killed when servers shut down, leaving paying customers with nothing. If a game is discontinued, players should have the right to an offline version or other viable access. This would protect consumers and safeguard our digital cultural heritage, as the Stop Killing Games initiative has highlighted.
